The reconstruction difficulty is determined by the distance between masked patches and visible ones. Patches with more visible patches around are generally easier to reconstruct.
Recovering patches with visible patches helps the model improve its local perception ability.
Reconstruction with few visible patches requires the model to have strong semantic reasoning ability due to limited neighboring information.
